title: Paloue coccinea
taxonomy: Paloue coccinea is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Sapindaceae. It belongs to the genus Paloue, which is a group of plants native to the Pacific Islands. The classification of Paloue coccinea is as follows: kingdom: Plantae, clade: Angiosperms, clade: Eudicots, clade: Rosids, order: Sapindales, family: Sapindaceae, genus: Paloue, species: P. coccinea. The exact classification may vary depending on the source, but it is generally accepted as a species within the Sapindaceae family. Further research may be necessary to confirm the exact classification.
uses: Paloue coccinea has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Its roots are often employed to create remedies for respiratory problems, such as bronchitis and coughs. The plant's leaves and stems are also believed to possess anti-inflammatory properties. Paloue coccinea is sometimes used to treat digestive issues, including diarrhea and stomach pain. However, its effectiveness for these uses has not been extensively scientifically studied.
